verses.text: Ephraim chides Gideon; he answers softly and calms them. Soft answer. Wisdom averts strife.
Ephraim chides Gideon; he answers softly and calms them. Soft answer. Wisdom averts strife.
Ephraim chides Gideon; he answers softly and calms them. Soft answer. Wisdom averts strife.
Succoth refuses bread to his weary men. Refused. Their own brethren fail them.
Succoth refuses bread to his weary men. Refused. Their own brethren fail them.
Succoth refuses bread to his weary men. Refused. Their own brethren fail them.
Succoth refuses bread to his weary men. Refused. Their own brethren fail them.
Penuel also refuses; Gideon warns them. Warned. He marks their scorn.
Penuel also refuses; Gideon warns them. Warned. He marks their scorn.
He pursues and takes the kings Zebah and Zalmunna. Captured. The foe is overtaken.
He pursues and takes the kings Zebah and Zalmunna. Captured. The foe is overtaken.
He pursues and takes the kings Zebah and Zalmunna. Captured. The foe is overtaken.
He punishes the men of Succoth and Penuel. Repaid. Their scorn is answered.
He punishes the men of Succoth and Penuel. Repaid. Their scorn is answered.
He punishes the men of Succoth and Penuel. Repaid. Their scorn is answered.
He punishes the men of Succoth and Penuel. Repaid. Their scorn is answered.
He punishes the men of Succoth and Penuel. Repaid. Their scorn is answered.
He slays the kings who had killed his brethren. Avenged. Justice is done.
He slays the kings who had killed his brethren. Avenged. Justice is done.
He slays the kings who had killed his brethren. Avenged. Justice is done.
He slays the kings who had killed his brethren. Avenged. Justice is done.
They offer him rule; he says, 'the LORD shall rule over you.' Refused. He will not be king.
They offer him rule; he says, 'the LORD shall rule over you.' Refused. He will not be king.
He makes an ephod of the spoil, which becomes a snare. The ephod. Even good men err.
He makes an ephod of the spoil, which becomes a snare. The ephod. Even good men err.
He makes an ephod of the spoil, which becomes a snare. The ephod. Even good men err.
He makes an ephod of the spoil, which becomes a snare. The ephod. Even good men err.
Midian is subdued; the land rests forty years; Gideon dies. Rest. The judge's work ends.
Midian is subdued; the land rests forty years; Gideon dies. Rest. The judge's work ends.
Midian is subdued; the land rests forty years; Gideon dies. Rest. The judge's work ends.
Midian is subdued; the land rests forty years; Gideon dies. Rest. The judge's work ends.
Midian is subdued; the land rests forty years; Gideon dies. Rest. The judge's work ends.
After his death, Israel turns again to Baal. Relapse. They forget their deliverer.
